questions.questionText: An angle at the circumference of a circle is 35°. What is the angle at the center subtended by the same arc?
questions.explanation: The angle at the center is twice the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc. So, 2 * 35° = 70°.

questions.questionText: A triangle is drawn inside a circle such that one of its sides is the diameter. What is the value of the angle opposite the diameter?
questions.explanation: The angle in a semicircle is always a right angle (90°).

questions.questionText: Points A, B, C, and D are on the circumference of a circle. $\angle BAC$ is 48°. Find the size of $\angle BDC$.
questions.explanation: Angles subtended by the same arc at the circumference (angles in the same segment) are equal. Both angles subtend arc BC.

questions.questionText: In a cyclic quadrilateral, one of the angles is 112°. What is the size of the opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral add up to 180°. So, 180° - 112° = 68°.

questions.questionText: A straight line touches a circle at a single point. What is the angle between this tangent and the radius at the point of contact?
questions.explanation: The angle between a tangent and a radius at the point of contact is always a right angle (90°).

questions.questionText: Two tangents are drawn from an external point P to a circle, touching at points A and B. If the length of tangent PA is 9 cm, what is the length of tangent PB?
questions.explanation: The lengths of the two tangents from an external point to a circle are equal.

questions.questionText: The angle subtended by a chord at the center of a circle is 80°. What is the angle subtended by the same chord at the circumference?
questions.explanation: The angle at the circumference is half the angle at the center subtended by the same arc. So, 80° / 2 = 40°.

questions.questionText: The opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are (x + 20)° and 80°. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°. So, (x + 20) + 80 = 180. This gives x + 100 = 180, so $x = 80$.

questions.questionText: The angle between a tangent and a chord through the point of contact is 52°. What is the angle in the alternate segment?
questions.explanation: The alternate segment theorem states that the angle between the tangent and a chord is equal to the angle in the alternate segment.

questions.questionText: O is the center of a circle. Points A and B are on the circumference. In triangle AOB, OA = OB. What is the reason for this?
options: Angles in the same segment are equal
questions.explanation: OA and OB are both lines from the center to the circumference, so they are both radii of the circle and must be equal in length.

questions.questionText: Points P, Q, and R are on a circle's circumference. If PQ is a diameter, what is the size of $\angle PRQ$?
questions.explanation: The angle subtended by a diameter at any point on the circumference (the angle in a semicircle) is always 90°.

questions.questionText: In a cyclic quadrilateral PQRS, angle P = 75°. Which other angle can you find?
questions.explanation: In a cyclic quadrilateral, opposite angles add up to 180°. Angle R is opposite to Angle P. So, Angle R = 180° - 75° = 105°.
